name: Game Show Specials
description: Questions designed for special round types — Snap, Switchagories, Grab Bag, Close Call, and Look Before You Leap
author: Game Night Crew

questions.question: Which of these is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean, covering more than 30% of Earth's surface — bigger than all landmasses combined!

questions.question: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars gets its red colour from iron oxide (rust) on its surface.

questions.question: In which year did the Berlin Wall fall?
questions.explanation: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989, marking a pivotal moment in ending the Cold War.

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.explanation: Au comes from the Latin word 'aurum' meaning gold.

questions.question: Which of these are landlocked countries? Select ALL correct answers.
questions.explanation: Switzerland, Bolivia, and Mongolia are all landlocked — no coastline at all!

questions.question: Which of these animals are mammals? Select ALL correct answers.
questions.explanation: Dolphins, bats, and whales are all mammals — they breathe air, are warm-blooded, and nurse their young.

questions.question: Rank these countries by population, from LARGEST to SMALLEST.
questions.explanation: Brazil (~215M), Indonesia (~275M)… wait — correct order: Indonesia (275M), Brazil (215M), Nigeria (220M), Russia (145M). Let me fix: Indonesia, Nigeria, Brazil, Russia.

questions.question: Rank these inventions from OLDEST to NEWEST.
This person was born in 1918 in Mvezo, studied law at the University of Fort Hare, was imprisoned for 27 years, and became the first Black president of South Africa. Who is it?
reveal_chunks: studied law at the University of Fort Hare,
and became the first Black president of South Africa.
questions.explanation: Nelson Mandela (Madiba) — the father of the Rainbow Nation.

questions.question: This element has atomic number 79, has been valued since ancient Egypt, is used in electronics and jewellery, and its chemical symbol comes from Latin. What element is it?
reveal_chunks: has been valued since ancient Egypt,
is used in electronics and jewellery,
and its chemical symbol comes from Latin.
questions.explanation: Gold (Au from 'aurum') — atomic number 79, one of the most prized elements in history.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a primary colour?
questions.explanation: In traditional colour theory (subtractive), the primary colours are red, blue, and yellow. Green is a secondary colour made by mixing blue and yellow.

questions.question: Which of these are Nobel Prize categories? Select ALL correct answers.
questions.explanation: Nobel Prizes are awarded in Physics, Chemistry, Literature, Peace, Physiology/Medicine, and Economic Sciences. There is no Nobel Prize for Mathematics or Engineering.

questions.question: Rank these planets by distance from the Sun, CLOSEST to FARTHEST.
questions.explanation: Mercury (closest), Venus, Mars, Jupiter (farthest of these four).

questions.question: Which country hosted the 2010 FIFA World Cup?
questions.explanation: South Africa hosted the 2010 FIFA World Cup — the first African nation to do so. Vuvuzelas forever!

questions.question: This company was founded in a garage in 1976, created the Macintosh computer, and is famous for the iPhone. Its logo is a fruit with a bite taken out. What company is it?
reveal_chunks: This company was founded in a garage in 1976,
and is famous for the iPhone.
Its logo is a fruit with a bite taken out.
questions.explanation: Apple Inc., founded by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne.
